title: santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. insularis
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. insularis, also known as the Jersey tea plant, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. insularis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and improve respiratory health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable addition to natural medicine.
